county_metadata.record_losses: Minor courthouse fire 1872 caused limited record loss. Most records survived. Some early court records damaged or lost.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1771 from Rowan and Orange counties. Pre-1771 records split between both parent counties. Quaker community (New Garden Meeting) settled here from 1750s — exceptional church records. Battle of Guilford Courthouse (1781) was a turning point of the Revolution.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1771. Guilford County has excellent online ROD portal. Quaker (Friends) meeting records are extraordinary — New Garden Monthly Meeting records from 1750s. Guilford County Genealogical Society is very active. Greensboro Historical Museum has local collections.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deeds and indexes from county formation. Continuous record despite minor 1872 fire.
Quaker meeting records from 1754 including birth/death registers, marriage certificates, minutes, and membership records. Among the most detailed church records in the NC Piedmont.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptional preservation — Quakers maintained meticulous records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1771 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Exceptional preservation for a colonial county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Edgecombe 1758. Important colonial and Revolutionary county — the Halifax Resolves (April 12, 1776) were the first formal action by any colony recommending independence. Parent of Nash (1777), Warren (via Bute), Northampton (1741 — actually formed before Halifax from Bertie). Pre-1758 records in Edgecombe. Halifax County deed books 1-6 contain Edgecombe Precinct deeds from 1729.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — exceptional colonial records. Deeds from 1729 (pre-county formation). Court records from 1758. Halifax County ROD has online portal. Historic Halifax State Historic Site has interpretive materials. For pre-1758, check Edgecombe County records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deed books 1-6 contain Edgecombe Precinct records from 1729 — among the oldest land records in NC. Continuous from 1758.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptional preservation. Pre-1758 records reflect Edgecombe Precinct jurisdiction.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation. Colonial-era wills particularly significant.
Marriage bonds from 1758 and registers from 1851. Colonial bonds are significant for eastern NC genealogy.
Court of Pleas and Quarter Sessions minutes from 1758. Colonial court records are extensive and well-preserved.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fires in 1892 and 1894 destroyed many court records. Deed and some will records survived. Marriage records have gaps.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Cumberland 1855. Pre-1855 records in Cumberland County. Cumberland has exceptional records from 1754, so pre-Harnett research is well-supported.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds (survived fire). Cumberland County ROD portal is excellent for pre-1855 research. Harnett County Genealogical Society has local publications. NC State Archives may have pre-fire materials deposited before the losses.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deeds and indexes from county formation. Survived courthouse fires.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts. Some records may have gaps from courthouse fires.
Marriage bonds and registers. Some gaps due to courthouse fires in 1892/1894.

source_profiles.source_name: Cumberland County ROD (pre-1855 Harnett area records)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: All pre-1855 Harnett County area records are in Cumberland County. Cumberland's ROD portal has fully digitized records from Book 1 (1754).

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Buncombe 1808. Pre-1808 records in Buncombe County. Parent of Macon (1828), Jackson (1851), Swain (1871), and indirectly Graham and Clay. Pre-formation land records from 1786 available (Buncombe-era grants in Haywood area).
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1808. Haywood County Historical and Genealogical Society is active. Good pre-formation land records from 1786. Census records from 1810. For Cherokee-related records, check federal Indian affairs records at NARA.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deeds and indexes from county formation. Some pre-formation land records from 1786 (Buncombe-era).
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1808 and registers from 1851.
Active society with publications, family files, and local history resources for mountain NC genealogy.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Buncombe 1838. Pre-1838 records in Buncombe County. Pre-formation land records from 1835 available.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1838. Henderson County Genealogical and Historical Society (HCGHS) is active. Good online ROD portal. Census records from 1840. For pre-1838, check Buncombe County records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deeds from 1835 (pre-formation) through 1960. Indexes included.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1838 and registers from 1851.
Active society with publications, cemetery records, and local history resources.

county_metadata.record_losses: SEVERE: Courthouse fire 1830 destroyed many early records. Union forces burned Winton February 1862 — one of the first towns destroyed in the Civil War. Between the two fires, most pre-1830 records and many 1830-1862 records were lost. One of NC's most damaged 'burned counties.' Creative research strategies required.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1759 from Chowan, Bertie, and Northampton counties. Pre-1759 records scattered across all three parent counties. The 1830 and 1862 fires make this one of the most challenging NC counties for genealogical research.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with whatever survives on FamilySearch. NC State Archives is critical for any surviving pre-fire materials. Check parent county records (Chowan, Bertie, Northampton) for pre-1759 research. Adjacent county records may fill gaps. Census records from 1790 are complete. DigitalNC newspapers can supplement lost courthouse records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: online portal — surviving records; many gaps due to fires

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Surviving deeds — significant gaps due to 1830 and 1862 fires. Post-1862 records are generally complete.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Pre-1830 deeds largely destroyed. 1830-1862 records partially lost in Civil War burning of Winton.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Surviving wills primarily from 1830 forward, with major gaps. Pre-1830 wills largely destroyed.

source_profiles.source_name: NC State Archives — Hertford County Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State Archives holdings are critical for this burned county. May contain pre-fire materials that were deposited before losses. Alternative records (bonds, miscellaneous) may bypass courthouse storage.
Post-Civil War marriage records. Pre-1862 marriage bonds destroyed in fires.
